toolUseResult.originalFile:
![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg) ![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json) ## What it does - **Discovers releases** by polling Docker Hub and GitHub on configurable intervals - **Routes notifications** to Slack, Discord, and webhooks the moment a new version lands - **Generates AI reports** via Google Gemini agents that research changelogs, assess risk, and summarize what changed - **Serves a dashboard** (Next.js) for managing projects, sources, subscriptions, and browsing releases in real time ## Architecture ```mermaid graph LR A["Ingestion
(polling)"] --> B["PostgreSQL +
River Queue"] B --> C["Agent
(ADK-Go)"] C --> D["Routing
(channels)"] A -.- A1["Docker Hub
GitHub"] B -.- B1["Transactional Outbox
LISTEN/NOTIFY → SSE"] C -.- C1["Gemini LLM
research tools"] D -.- D1["Slack · Discord
Webhooks"] ``` Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction — zero-loss guarantee. See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md), [API.md](API.md), and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. ## Quick start **Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ ```bash # Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) make dev # In another terminal — start the frontend make frontend-install make frontend-dev ``` The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. ## Environment variables | Variable | Default | Purpose | |----------|---------|---------| | `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | | `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | | `NO_AUTH` | _(unset)_ | Set to `true` to disable API key auth (development) | | `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` | | `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` | | `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` / `gpt-5.2` | Model name (default depends on provider) | | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=gemini`) | | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | OpenAI API key (required when `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`) | | `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API base URL | | `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` | `gpt-5-search-api` | Search-capable model for OpenAI web search sub-agent | ## Project structure ``` cmd/ server/ Entry point — wires all layers together agent/ Agent CLI — run agent analysis for a project internal/ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker openai/ OpenAI-compatible LLM provider api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth db/ Connection pool and migrations ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub Atom) models/ Shared domain types queue/ River job definitions and client routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) scripts/ Integration test harness ``` ## Extending More providers (npm, PyPI, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, email, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: **Add a registry provider** — implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: ```go type IIngestionSource interface { FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) } ``` **Add a notification channel** — implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: ```go type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error } ``` ## Useful commands ```bash make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server make test # go test ./... make vet # go vet ./... make lint # alias for vet make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database make frontend-build # build Next.js static export make agent-dev # run agent CLI for a specific project make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes ```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Design: Migrate OpenAI Adapter to Responses API

**Date:** 2026-03-04
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

The current OpenAI adapter uses the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) with `web_search_options: {}` for web search. This has limitations:

- Requires a dedicated search-capable model (`gpt-5-search-api`) — separate from the main model
- No control over search parameters (domain filtering, search context size, user location)
- Chat Completions search models are being superseded by the Responses API

## Solution

Migrate the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`), which:

- Supports `web_search` as a native tool alongside function tools
- Works with any supported model (gpt-5, gpt-4.1, o4-mini, etc.)
- Provides domain filtering (`allowed_domains`), `search_context_size`, and `user_location`
- Eliminates the need for a separate search model

## Scope

### Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `internal/agent/openai/openai.go` | Rewrite request/response types and `doRequest()` for Responses API |
| `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go` | Update tests for new wire format |
| `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go` | Add configurable fields (SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains) |
| `internal/agent/model.go` | Remove `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` |
| `internal/agent/orchestrator.go` | Simplify search sub-agent (same model, no separate model creation) |
| `cmd/server/main.go` | Remove `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var |

### Request Format (Before → After)

**Before (Chat Completions):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "get_releases", ...}}],
  "web_search_options": {}
}
```

**After (Responses):**
```json
{
  "model": "gpt-5.2",
  "input": [{"role": "user", "content": "..."}],
  "tools": [
    {"type": "function", "name": "get_releases", "parameters": {...}},
    {"type": "web_search", "search_context_size": "medium"}
  ]
}
```

### Response Format (Before → After)

**Before:** `choices[0].message.content` + `choices[0].message.tool_calls`

**After:** `output` array containing:
- `{type: "web_search_call", status: "completed"}` — search invocation record
- `{type: "function_call", id, name, arguments}` — function tool calls
- `{type: "message", content: [{type: "output_text", text, annotations}]}` — final text with citations

### WebSearch Tool Enhancement

```go
type WebSearch struct {
    SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium" (default), "high"
    AllowedDomains    []string // optional domain filter
}
```

The `ProcessRequest` method will encode these fields into the tool config rather than using the GoogleSearch marker hack.

### Config Simplification

**Removed from LLMConfig:**
- `OpenAISearchModel` — no longer needed; any model supports web search via tools

**Removed env vars:**
- `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` — search model is the same as the main model

### Orchestrator Simplification

The search sub-agent no longer needs a separate model. In `BuildAgent()`:

```go
// Before: separate model for search
searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, LLMConfig{
    Provider: "openai", Model: "gpt-5-search-api", ...
})

// After: same model, web search is just a tool
searchAgent = llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
    Model: llmModel,  // same model as root/data agent
    Tools: []tool.Tool{oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}},
})
```

## Non-Goals

- Streaming support (not implemented today, stays that way)
- Deep research models (o3-deep-research etc.)
- Gemini adapter changes (unaffected)

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/pc/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/4.3.1/skills/verification-before-completion

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.
Running fresh verification now.

content.text:
All tests pass (exit 0), vet clean (exit 0). The OpenAI Responses API migration is fully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

**Migrated** the OpenAI adapter from Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`).

### Key commits:
- `ac97603` — Core adapter rewrite: new types (`responsesRequest`, `inputItem`, `responseTool`, etc.), new conversion functions (`convertInputs`, `convertResponseTools`, `convertResponseOutput`), `doRequest` now POSTs to `/responses`
- `f539e72` — Cleanup: removed dead `mapFinishReason`, fixed doc comment
- `17acf32` — Removed `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig`, simplified `BuildAgent` to use same model for search
- `b53b05f` — Removed stale `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` from README
- `dea3701` — Marked design doc as implemented

### What changed:
| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) | Responses API (`/v1/responses`) |
| Separate search model (`gpt-5-search-api`) | Same model with `web_search` tool |
| `web_search_options: {}` (no control) | `{type: "web_search", search_context_size, filters}` |
| `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var | Removed — not needed |
| `WebSearch{}` (no config) | `WebSearch{SearchContextSize, AllowedDomains}` |

content.text:
Found it. Line 75: `agentpkg.BuildAgent(ctx, store, project, llmConfig, "")` — the version is hardcoded as empty string `""`. Then in `BuildAgent`, `strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)` replaces all `{{VERSION}}` placeholders with empty string, producing instructions like "analyzing version  of a software project" and "Focus ONLY on version ." — which confuses the LLM.

This isn't a regression from our changes — this was pre-existing. But let me check if there's a version flag that should exist.

content.content: No matches found

content.text:
There's no `--version` flag on the agent CLI. The dev CLI passes `""` as the version to `BuildAgent`, which causes the `{{VERSION}}` placeholders to become empty strings in the agent instruction. The LLM then sees "analyzing version  of a software project" and gets confused.

The fix is straightforward — add a `--version` flag to the agent CLI.

	flag.Parse()
input.new_string:
projectID := flag.String("project-id", "", "Project ID to scope the agent to (required)")
	version := flag.String("version", "", "Target version to analyze (e.g. v1.10.15)")
	flag.Parse()

file.content:
const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.

Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.

Use the available tools to:
1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).

CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting — just the raw JSON.

For download_links: prefer direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) over generic release pages. Include the general release page URL as well, but prioritize direct download links when available.

The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
{
  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",

The instruction is version-centric — every sentence references `{{VERSION}}`. When version is empty, it becomes nonsensical. The production path (`executeAgent`) also has this issue but handles it differently — it falls back to "Analyze the recent releases" as the user prompt (line 325), but the system instruction still has empty placeholders.

The cleanest fix: when version is empty, use an alternative instruction that doesn't reference a specific version.
The fix should be in `BuildAgent` — when version is empty, use a generic instruction instead of one riddled with empty `{{VERSION}}` placeholders.

message.content: why the search_agent doesn't get invoked by default?

content.text: Let me investigate how the search agent is set up and why it might not be getting invoked.

The issue is in the search agent's description at line 132:

```
"Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources"
```

And the DefaultInstruction at line 55:

```
5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
```

Both explicitly tell the LLM to treat web search as a last resort. The root agent sees two tools — `data_agent` and `search_agent` — and the descriptions actively discourage search usage. The LLM reads "ONLY when" and almost never decides it's needed.

The fix is to make the instruction and description encourage search use rather than gatekeep it.

const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.

Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.

Use the available tools to:
1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources
   (e.g., community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues).

CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting — just the raw JSON.

For download_links: prefer direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) over generic release pages. Include the general release page URL as well, but prioritize direct download links when available.

The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
{
  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
}`

Key files modified:

- `internal/agent/openai/websearch.go` - Added SearchContextSize and AllowedDomains fields
- `internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go` - New tests for conversion functions

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user initially requested wrapping the OpenAI search tool into a standard tool or using a sub-agent, referencing `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/guides/tools-web-search/`. Through brainstorming, the intent was refined to: **migrate the OpenAI adapter from the Chat Completions API (`/v1/chat/completions`) to the Responses API (`/v1/responses`)** to gain native `web_search` tool support with domain filtering, search context size control, and elimination of the separate search model requirement. After implementation, the user reported two bugs: (1) the agent-dev CLI was broken due to empty version placeholders, and (2) the `search_agent` never gets invoked by default.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Google ADK-Go v0.5.0**: Agent framework implementing `model.LLM` interface with `GenerateContent(ctx, req, streaming) iter.Seq2[*model.LLMResponse, error]`
   - **Multi-agent orchestration**: Root agent (`release_analyst`) delegates to `data_agent` (DB queries) and `search_agent` (web search) via `agenttool.New()`
   - **OpenAI Responses API**: Uses `POST /v1/responses` with `input` items (not `messages`), flat tool definitions (not nested `function` key), `function_call`/`function_call_output` input items for tool results
   - **OpenAI Chat Completions API** (old): `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `messages`, `web_search_options: {}`, required special search models like `gpt-5-search-api`
   - **GoogleSearch marker pattern**: ADK tools use `ProcessRequest` to inject `genai.GoogleSearch{}` markers into `req.Config.Tools`, which the model wrapper detects and translates to provider-specific search config
   - **River job queue**: Agent runs are triggered via transactional outbox pattern with `AgentJobArgs`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`internal/agent/openai/openai.go`** — Core OpenAI adapter implementing `model.LLM`. Completely rewritten from Chat Completions to Responses API.
     - Removed: `chatRequest`, `chatMessage`, `chatTool`, `chatFunction`, `chatResponse`, `chatChoice`, `chatUsage`, `webSearchOptions`, `toolCall`, `functionCall`, `convertContents`, `convertTools`, `convertResponse`, `hasWebSearch`, `mergeAssistantToolCalls`, `mapFinishReason`
     - Added: `responsesRequest`, `inputItem`, `responseTool`, `searchFilters`, `responsesResponse`, `outputItem`, `outputContent`, `annotation`, `respUsage`, `apiError`, `convertInputs`, `convertResponseTools`, `convertResponseOutput`
     - `NewModel` signature changed from 3 to 4 params: `NewModel(_ context.Context, modelName string, cfg Config, wsConfig *WebSearch) (model.LLM, error)`
     - `openaiModel` struct gained `wsConfig *WebSearch` field
     - `doRequest` now POSTs to `m.baseURL+"/responses"` instead of `/chat/completions`
     - Key conversion: ADK `FunctionCall` parts → `{type: "function_call", call_id, name, arguments}` input items; ADK `FunctionResponse` parts → `{type: "function_call_output", call_id, output}` input items
     ```go
     func convertInputs(req *model.LLMRequest) ([]inputItem, string) {
         var items []inputItem
         var instructions string
         if req.Config != nil && req.Config.SystemInstruction != nil {
             var text string
             for _, p := range req.Config.SystemInstruction.Parts {
                 if p.Text != "" { text += p.Text }
             }
             instructions = text
         }
         for _, c := range req.Contents {
             for _, p := range c.Parts {
                 switch {
                 case p.FunctionCall != nil:
                     argsJSON, _ := json.Marshal(p.FunctionCall.Args)
                     items = append(items, inputItem{Type: "function_call", CallID: p.FunctionCall.ID, Name: p.FunctionCall.Name, Arguments: string(argsJSON)})
                 case p.FunctionResponse != nil:
                     respJSON, _ := json.Marshal(p.FunctionResponse.Response)
                     items = append(items, inputItem{Type: "function_call_output", CallID: p.FunctionResponse.ID, Output: string(respJSON)})
                 case p.Text != "":
                     items = append(items, inputItem{Type: "message", Role: mapRole(c.Role), Content: p.Text})
                 }
             }
         }
         return items, instructions
     }
     ```

   - **`internal/agent/openai/websearch.go`** — WebSearch tool definition. Added config fields.
     ```go
     type WebSearch struct {
         SearchContextSize string   // "low", "medium", or "high"
         AllowedDomains    []string // restrict search to these domains
     }
     ```

   - **`internal/agent/openai/openai_test.go`** — Tests for the adapter.
     - Removed: `TestConvertTools_SkipsGoogleSearchMarker`, `TestHasWebSearch`
     - Added: `TestConvertInputs_SystemInstruction`, `TestConvertInputs_FunctionCallAndResponse`, `TestConvertResponseTools_WebSearch`, `TestConvertResponseOutput_TextAndFunctionCalls`
     - Kept: `TestSchemaToMap_LowercasesType`

   - **`internal/agent/openai/websearch_test.go`** — Tests for WebSearch tool. Renamed `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest` → `TestWebSearch_ProcessRequest_SetsMarker`.

   - **`internal/agent/model.go`** — LLM provider factory. Removed `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig`, added `wsConfig` parameter.
     ```go
     type LLMConfig struct {
         Provider     string
         Model        string
         GoogleAPIKey string
         OpenAIAPIKey  string
         OpenAIBaseURL string
     }
     func NewLLMModel(ctx context.Context, cfg LLMConfig, wsConfig *oaimodel.WebSearch) (model.LLM, error) {
     ```

   - **`internal/agent/orchestrator.go`** — Agent lifecycle orchestration. Simplified `BuildAgent` to use same model for search, fixed empty version handling.
     ```go
     // Search sub-agent now uses same model with web search enabled
     case "openai":
         wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
         searchTool = *wsConfig
         searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
     ```
     ```go
     // Empty version handling
     if version != "" {
         instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
     } else {
         instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
         instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
     }
     ```
     - The `DefaultInstruction` at line 55 says: `"5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"`
     - The search agent description at line 132 says: `"Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources"`

   - **`cmd/agent/main.go`** — Agent dev CLI. Added `--version` flag, passes `*version` to `BuildAgent`.

   - **`cmd/server/main.go`** — Server entrypoint. Removed `OpenAISearchModel` from `LLMConfig` initialization.

   - **`README.md`** — Removed stale `OPENAI_SEARCH_MODEL` env var documentation.

content.text:
The two culprits:

1. **Line 55**: `"5. Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"`
2. **Line 132**: `"Search the web for additional context about a release. Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources, such as community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption statistics, or known issues."`

Both use restrictive "ONLY when" phrasing that tells the LLM to treat search as a last resort. The fix is to make search a default step in the analysis workflow, not a conditional one.

content.text: Now let me verify the build still passes.

Build passes. Here's what was changed and why:

**Root cause:** The `DefaultInstruction` and search agent `Description` both used "ONLY when" language, which tells the LLM to treat web search as a last resort. Since the root agent decides whether to delegate to sub-agents based on their descriptions and the system instruction, this phrasing caused it to almost never invoke `search_agent`.

**Fix (two lines in `orchestrator.go`):**

1. **Instruction (line 55):** Changed from _"Use web search ONLY when you need additional context not available from sources"_ → _"Always search the web for supplementary context about this release"_ — makes search a default step, not a conditional one.

2. **Search agent description (line 132):** Changed from _"Use this ONLY when you need information not available from the project's sources"_ → _"Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights"_ — tells the root agent to always delegate to search.

content.text:
Build passes. The fix explicitly tells the LLM what `download_links` should and should not contain:

- **Should:** direct binary/artifact download URLs, GitHub/project release page URLs
- **Should not:** CVE links, security advisory URLs (nvd.nist.gov, osv.dev), blog posts, documentation

The previous instruction only said "prefer" direct downloads but never excluded non-download URLs, so the LLM treated any URL it found as fair game for that field.

content.text:
The search agent doesn't know what project it's analyzing — it's searching the web for "v3.0.8" without any project context, so it gets ambiguous results across unrelated projects. The search agent needs the project name in its instruction.

Let me check how the project name is available.

The search agent has no `Instruction` set, so it has no idea which project it's working on. When the root agent delegates "search for v3.0.8", the search agent just blindly searches the web for "v3.0.8" with no project context. The fix is to give the search agent an instruction that includes the project name.

	})

content.text:
Build passes. The issue was that the `search_agent` had no `Instruction` — it didn't know which project it was analyzing. When the root agent asked it to search for "v3.0.8", it searched blindly and got ambiguous results from unrelated projects.

The fix adds a project-scoped instruction: `"You are a web search assistant for the project "<name>". Always include the project name in your search queries..."` — so now it will search for e.g. "Prysm v3.0.8" instead of just "v3.0.8".

    29→
    30→// OrchestratorStore defines all data access methods required by the agent
    31→// orchestrator to load project configuration, run the agent, and persist
    32→// the resulting semantic release.
    33→type OrchestratorStore interface {
    34→	AgentDataStore
    35→	GetProject(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Project, error)
    36→	GetAgentRun(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.AgentRun, error)
    37→	UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx context.Context, id, status string) error
    38→	CreateSemanticRelease(ctx context.Context, sr *models.SemanticRelease, releaseIDs []string) error
    39→	UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx context.Context, id string, semanticReleaseID string) error
    40→	ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx context.Context, projectID string) ([]models.Subscription, error)
    41→	GetChannel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.NotificationChannel, error)
    42→	ListSourcesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.Source, int, error)
    43→	HasReleaseForVersion(ctx context.Context, sourceID, version string) (bool, error)
    44→}
    45→
    46→const DefaultInstruction = `You are a release intelligence agent analyzing version {{VERSION}} of a software project.
    47→
    48→Focus ONLY on version {{VERSION}}. Cross-check this version across all available sources.
    49→
    50→Use the available tools to:
    51→1. Fetch releases and find the one matching {{VERSION}} from each source.
    52→2. Inspect release details (changelogs, commit data, raw payloads) for {{VERSION}} only.
    53→3. Check binary/image availability directly from the source data.
    54→4. Review the project's context sources (runbooks, documentation) for relevant background.
    55→5. Always search the web for supplementary context about this release
    56→   (community sentiment, security advisories, network adoption stats, known issues, migration guides).
    57→
    58→CRITICAL: Your final response MUST be a single JSON object and nothing else.
    59→Do not include any explanation, commentary, or markdown formatting — just the raw JSON.
    60→
    61→For download_links: ONLY include URLs where the user can actually download the software — direct binary/artifact URLs for specific platforms (e.g., linux-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64 .tar.gz/.zip files) and the GitHub/project release page. Do NOT include CVE links, security advisory URLs, blog posts, documentation, or any non-download URLs.
    62→
    63→The JSON object must have exactly these fields:
    64→{
    65→  "subject": "Ready to Deploy: <Project> <Version> (<Urgency Summary>)",
    66→  "urgency": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
    67→  "urgency_reason": "Why this urgency level (e.g., 'Hard Fork detected in Discord #announcements')",
    68→  "status_checks": ["Docker Image Verified", "Binaries Available"],
    69→  "changelog_summary": "One-line summary of key changes (e.g., 'Fixes sync bug in block 14,000,000')",
    70→  "availability": "GA|RC|Beta",
    71→  "adoption": "Percentage or recommendation (e.g., '12% of network updated (Wait recommended if not urgent)')",
    72→  "recommendation": "Actionable 1-2 sentence recommendation for the SRE team",
    73→  "download_commands": ["docker pull ethereum/client-go:v1.10.15"],
    74→  "download_links": ["https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-linux-amd64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://gethstore.blob.core.windows.net/builds/geth-darwin-arm64-1.10.15-8be800ff.tar.gz", "https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/releases/tag/v1.10.15"]
    75→}`
    76→
    77→// BuildAgent creates the ADK-Go LLM agent for a given project. This is used
    78→// by both the production orchestrator and the dev entrypoint to ensure the
    79→// same agent configuration is used everywhere.
    80→func BuildAgent(ctx context.Context, store AgentDataStore, project *models.Project, llmConfig LLMConfig, version string) (agent.Agent, error) {
    81→	instruction := DefaultInstruction
    82→	if project.AgentPrompt != "" {
    83→		instruction = project.AgentPrompt + "\n\n" + instruction
    84→	}
    85→	if version != "" {
    86→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", version)
    87→	} else {
    88→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "version {{VERSION}} of", "the latest releases of")
    89→		instruction = strings.ReplaceAll(instruction, "{{VERSION}}", "the latest version")
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	llmModel, err := NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, nil)
    93→	if err != nil {
    94→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create LLM model: %w", err)
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	// Create project-scoped function tools.
    98→	functionTools, err := NewTools(store, project.ID)
    99→	if err != nil {
   100→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create agent tools: %w", err)
   101→	}
   102→
   103→	// Data sub-agent: handles DB queries for releases and context sources.
   104→	dataAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   105→		Name:        "data_agent",
   106→		Description: "Query project releases and context sources from the database. Use this to fetch release lists, release details, and context sources like runbooks and documentation.",
   107→		Model:       llmModel,
   108→		Tools:       functionTools,
   109→	})
   110→	if err != nil {
   111→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create data sub-agent: %w", err)
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// Search sub-agent: provider-specific web search.
   115→	var searchTool tool.Tool
   116→	var searchModel model.LLM
   117→	switch llmConfig.Provider {
   118→	case "openai":
   119→		wsConfig := &oaimodel.WebSearch{SearchContextSize: "medium"}
   120→		searchTool = *wsConfig
   121→		searchModel, err = NewLLMModel(ctx, llmConfig, wsConfig)
   122→		if err != nil {
   123→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("create OpenAI search model: %w", err)
   124→		}
   125→	default: // gemini
   126→		searchTool = geminitool.GoogleSearch{}
   127→		searchModel = llmModel
   128→	}
   129→
   130→	searchInstruction := fmt.Sprintf(
   131→		"You are a web search assistant for the project %q. Always include the project name in your search queries to get relevant results. Focus on this specific project — ignore results for unrelated projects that happen to share the same version number.",
   132→		project.Name,
   133→	)
   134→
   135→	searchAgent, err := llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   136→		Name:        "search_agent",
   137→		Description: "Search the web for context about a release — community sentiment, security advisories, adoption statistics, known issues, and migration guides. Use this for every release analysis to enrich the report with external insights.",
   138→		Instruction: searchInstruction,
   139→		Model:       searchModel,
   140→		Tools:       []tool.Tool{searchTool},
   141→	})
   142→	if err != nil {
   143→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create search sub-agent: %w", err)
   144→	}
   145→
   146→	// Root agent orchestrates data lookup and web search via sub-agents.
   147→	return llmagent.New(llmagent.Config{
   148→		Name:        "release_analyst",
   149→		Description: "Analyzes upstream releases and produces semantic release reports.",
   150→		Model:       llmModel,
   151→		Instruction: instruction,
   152→		Tools: []tool.Tool{
   153→			agenttool.New(dataAgent, nil),
   154→			agenttool.New(searchAgent, nil),
   155→		},
   156→	})
   157→}
   158→
   159→// checkAllSourcesReady returns true if every source in the project has a
   160→// release matching the target version.
   161→func (o *Orchestrator) checkAllSourcesReady(ctx context.Context, projectID, version string) (bool, error) {
   162→	// Use a high limit to fetch all sources; projects rarely exceed a handful.
   163→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, projectID, 1, 1000)
   164→	if err != nil {
   165→		return false, fmt.Errorf("list sources: %w", err)
   166→	}
   167→	if len(sources) == 0 {
   168→		return true, nil
   169→	}
   170→
   171→	for _, src := range sources {
   172→		has, err := o.store.HasReleaseForVersion(ctx, src.ID, version)
   173→		if err != nil {
   174→			return false, fmt.Errorf("check source %s: %w", src.ID, err)
   175→		}
   176→		if !has {
   177→			slog.Info("agent: source not ready for version",
   178→				"project_id", projectID,
   179→				"source_id", src.ID,
   180→				"version", version,
   181→			)
   182→			return false, nil
   183→		}
   184→	}
   185→	return true, nil
   186→}
   187→
   188→// Orchestrator manages the lifecycle of an agent run: loading project config,
   189→// creating an ADK-Go agent with project-specific tools and instructions,
   190→// running the agent, and persisting the result as a semantic release.
   191→type Orchestrator struct {
   192→	store     OrchestratorStore
   193→	llmConfig LLMConfig
   194→	publicURL string // base URL for internal Changelogue links (e.g. "https://changelogue.example.com")
   195→}
   196→
   197→// NewOrchestrator creates a new Orchestrator. It validates that the provided
   198→// LLMConfig has the required API key for the configured provider. If the key
   199→// is missing, it returns an error so the caller can degrade gracefully.
   200→func NewOrchestrator(store OrchestratorStore, cfg LLMConfig, publicURL string) (*Orchestrator, error) {
   201→	switch cfg.Provider {
   202→	case "openai":
   203→		if cfg.OpenAIAPIKey == "" {
   204→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("OPENAI_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   205→		}
   206→	default: // gemini
   207→		if cfg.GoogleAPIKey == "" {
   208→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("GOOGLE_API_KEY is not set; agent orchestrator requires an LLM API key")
   209→		}
   210→	}
   211→	return &Orchestrator{
   212→		store:     store,
   213→		llmConfig: cfg,
   214→		publicURL: strings.TrimRight(publicURL, "/"),
   215→	}, nil
   216→}
   217→
   218→// RunAgent executes the full agent lifecycle for a given agent run:
   219→//  1. Marks the run as "running".
   220→//  2. Loads the project and builds the agent prompt.
   221→//  3. Creates an ADK-Go LLM agent with project-scoped tools.
   222→//  4. Runs the agent and captures its final text response.
   223→//  5. Parses the response into a SemanticReport.
   224→//  6. Creates a SemanticRelease and links it to the agent run.
   225→//  7. Marks the run as "completed" (or "failed" on error).
   226→func (o *Orchestrator) RunAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) error {
   227→	// Mark as running.
   228→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "running"); err != nil {
   229→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to running: %w", err)
   230→	}
   231→
   232→	// Execute the agent; capture any error to mark the run as failed.
   233→	result, err := o.executeAgent(ctx, run)
   234→	if err != nil {
   235→		slog.Error("agent run failed", "run_id", run.ID, "err", err)
   236→		// Best-effort: mark the run as failed.
   237→		if statusErr := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "failed"); statusErr != nil {
   238→			slog.Error("agent: failed to mark run as failed",
   239→				"run_id", run.ID, "status_err", statusErr)
   240→		}
   241→		return err
   242→	}
   243→
   244→	// Link the semantic release to the agent run and mark completed.
   245→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunResult(ctx, run.ID, result.semanticReleaseID); err != nil {
   246→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run result: %w", err)
   247→	}
   248→	if err := o.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "completed"); err != nil {
   249→		return fmt.Errorf("update agent run status to completed: %w", err)
   250→	}
   251→
   252→	// Send project-level notifications (best-effort; errors are logged, not returned).
   253→	o.sendProjectNotifications(ctx, run, result)
   254→
   255→	return nil
   256→}
   257→
   258→// agentResult holds the output of a successful agent execution, used to
   259→// construct notifications after the semantic release is persisted.
   260→type agentResult struct {
   261→	semanticReleaseID string
   262→	version           string
   263→	reportText        string
   264→	projectName       string
   265→}
   266→
   267→// executeAgent performs the actual LLM agent interaction and returns the
   268→// agentResult containing the semantic release ID and metadata for notifications.
   269→func (o *Orchestrator) executeAgent(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun) (*agentResult, error) {
   270→	// Load project.
   271→	slog.Info("agent: loading project", "run_id", run.ID, "project_id", run.ProjectID)
   272→	project, err := o.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   273→	if err != nil {
   274→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
   275→	}
   276→	slog.Info("agent: project loaded", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name)
   277→
   278→	// Extract target version from the agent run.
   279→	version := run.Version
   280→	if version == "" {
   281→		// Fallback: parse from trigger "auto:version:v1.10.15"
   282→		if strings.HasPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:") {
   283→			version = strings.TrimPrefix(run.Trigger, "auto:version:")
   284→		}
   285→	}
   286→
   287→	// Build the agent using the shared constructor.
   288→	slog.Info("agent: building agent", "run_id", run.ID,
   289→		"provider", o.llmConfig.Provider, "model", o.llmConfig.Model,
   290→		"version", version)
   291→	agentInstance, err := BuildAgent(ctx, o.store, project, o.llmConfig, version)
   292→	if err != nil {
   293→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("build agent: %w", err)
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	// Create in-memory session service and a new session.
   297→	sessionService := session.InMemoryService()
   298→	createResp, err := sessionService.Create(ctx, &session.CreateRequest{
   299→		AppName: "changelogue",
   300→		UserID:  "system",
   301→	})
   302→	if err != nil {
   303→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create session: %w", err)
   304→	}
   305→	sess := createResp.Session
   306→
   307→	// Create the runner with logging plugin for agent trace visibility.
   308→	r, err := runner.New(runner.Config{
   309→		AppName:        "changelogue",
   310→		Agent:          agentInstance,
   311→		SessionService: sessionService,
   312→		PluginConfig: runner.PluginConfig{
   313→			Plugins:      []*plugin.Plugin{loggingplugin.MustNew("agent_trace")},
   314→			CloseTimeout: 5 * time.Second,
   315→		},
   316→	})
   317→	if err != nil {
   318→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create runner: %w", err)
   319→	}
   320→
   321→	// Run the agent with a prompt requesting analysis.
   322→	slog.Info("agent: starting LLM run", "run_id", run.ID, "project", project.Name, "version", version)
   323→	var userPrompt string
   324→	if version != "" {
   325→		userPrompt = fmt.Sprintf("Analyze version %s for this project. Cross-check all sources and produce a semantic release report.", version)
   326→	} else {
   327→		userPrompt = "Analyze the recent releases for this project and produce a semantic release report."
   328→	}
   329→	userMsg := genai.NewContentFromText(userPrompt, "user")
   330→
   331→	var finalText string
   332→	eventCount := 0
   333→	for event, err := range r.Run(ctx, "system", sess.ID(), userMsg, agent.RunConfig{}) {
   334→		if err != nil {
   335→			slog.Error("agent: run event error",
   336→				"run_id", run.ID,
   337→				"event_count", eventCount,
   338→				"err", err,
   339→			)
   340→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent run event error: %w", err)
   341→		}
   342→		eventCount++
   343→		if event != nil && event.IsFinalResponse() && event.Content != nil {
   344→			for _, part := range event.Content.Parts {
   345→				if part.Text != "" {
   346→					finalText += part.Text
   347→				}
   348→			}
   349→		}
   350→	}
   351→	slog.Info("agent: LLM run finished",
   352→		"run_id", run.ID,
   353→		"event_count", eventCount,
   354→		"output_length", len(finalText),
   355→	)
   356→
   357→	if finalText == "" {
   358→		slog.Error("agent: produced no output",
   359→			"run_id", run.ID,
   360→			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   361→			"event_count", eventCount,
   362→		)
   363→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent produced no output")
   364→	}
   365→
   366→	// Parse the agent's response into a SemanticReport.
   367→	report, err := parseReport(finalText)
   368→	if err != nil {
   369→		slog.Warn("agent output was not valid JSON report, storing raw",
   370→			"run_id", run.ID, "parse_err", err, "raw_output", finalText)
   371→		// Fall back to storing the raw text as the summary.
   372→		report = &models.SemanticReport{
   373→			Summary: finalText,
   374→		}
   375→	}
   376→
   377→	reportJSON, err := json.Marshal(report)
   378→	if err != nil {
   379→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal report: %w", err)
   380→	}
   381→
   382→	// Gather release IDs for the semantic_release_sources join table.
   383→	// Only link releases that match the target version.
   384→	releases, _, err := o.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 200, false)
   385→	if err != nil {
   386→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("list releases for semantic release: %w", err)
   387→	}
   388→	releaseIDs := make([]string, 0, len(releases))
   389→	for _, r := range releases {
   390→		if r.Version == version {
   391→			releaseIDs = append(releaseIDs, r.ID)
   392→		}
   393→	}
   394→
   395→	// Use the target version for the semantic release.
   396→	srVersion := version
   397→	if srVersion == "" {
   398→		srVersion = "unknown"
   399→		if len(releases) > 0 {
   400→			srVersion = releases[0].Version
   401→		}
   402→	}
   403→
   404→	now := time.Now()
   405→	sr := &models.SemanticRelease{
   406→		ProjectID:   run.ProjectID,
   407→		Version:     srVersion,
   408→		Report:      reportJSON,
   409→		Status:      "completed",
   410→		CompletedAt: &now,
   411→	}
   412→
   413→	if err := o.store.CreateSemanticRelease(ctx, sr, releaseIDs); err != nil {
   414→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create semantic release: %w", err)
   415→	}
   416→
   417→	slog.Info("agent run produced semantic release",
   418→		"run_id", run.ID,
   419→		"semantic_release_id", sr.ID,
   420→		"version", sr.Version,
   421→	)
   422→
   423→	return &agentResult{
   424→		semanticReleaseID: sr.ID,
   425→		version:           srVersion,
   426→		reportText:        finalText,
   427→		projectName:       project.Name,
   428→	}, nil
   429→}
   430→
   431→// defaultSenders returns a map of channel type to Sender for the supported
   432→// notification channel types (webhook, slack, discord).
   433→func defaultSenders() map[string]routing.Sender {
   434→	httpClient := &http.Client{Timeout: 10 * time.Second}
   435→	return map[string]routing.Sender{
   436→		"webhook": &routing.WebhookSender{Client: httpClient},
   437→		"slack":   &routing.SlackSender{Client: httpClient},
   438→		"discord": &routing.DiscordSender{Client: httpClient},
   439→	}
   440→}
   441→
   442→// sendProjectNotifications looks up project-level subscriptions and sends a
   443→// notification to each channel with the semantic release report. This is
   444→// best-effort: errors are logged but do not propagate to the caller.
   445→func (o *Orchestrator) sendProjectNotifications(ctx context.Context, run *models.AgentRun, result *agentResult) {
   446→	subs, err := o.store.ListProjectSubscriptions(ctx, run.ProjectID)
   447→	if err != nil {
   448→		slog.Error("list project subscriptions for notification",
   449→			"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
   450→		return
   451→	}
   452→	if len(subs) == 0 {
   453→		slog.Debug("agent: no project subscriptions configured",
   454→			"project_id", run.ProjectID)
   455→		return
   456→	}
   457→
   458→	senders := defaultSenders()
   459→
   460→	msg := routing.Notification{
   461→		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("Semantic Release Report: %s %s", result.projectName, result.version),
   462→		Body:        result.reportText,
   463→		Version:     result.version,
   464→		ProjectName: result.projectName,
   465→	}
   466→
   467→	// Build ReleaseURL for the semantic release detail page.
   468→	if o.publicURL != "" {
   469→		msg.ReleaseURL = fmt.Sprintf("%s/projects/%s/semantic-releases/%s", o.publicURL, run.ProjectID, result.semanticReleaseID)
   470→	}
   471→
   472→	// Look up the first source for provider/repo info to build SourceURL.
   473→	sources, _, err := o.store.ListSourcesByProject(ctx, run.ProjectID, 1, 1)
   474→	if err == nil && len(sources) > 0 {
   475→		msg.Provider = sources[0].Provider
   476→		msg.Repository = sources[0].Repository
   477→		msg.SourceURL = routing.ProviderURL(sources[0].Provider, sources[0].Repository, result.version)
   478→	}
   479→
   480→	for _, sub := range subs {
   481→		ch, err := o.store.GetChannel(ctx, sub.ChannelID)
   482→		if err != nil {
   483→			slog.Error("get channel for project notification",
   484→				"channel_id", sub.ChannelID, "err", err)
   485→			continue
   486→		}
   487→
   488→		sender, ok := senders[ch.Type]
   489→		if !ok {
   490→			slog.Warn("unknown channel type for project notification", "type", ch.Type)
   491→			continue
   492→		}
   493→
   494→		if err := sender.Send(ctx, ch, msg); err != nil {
   495→			slog.Error("send project notification failed",
   496→				"channel", ch.Name, "type", ch.Type, "err", err)
   497→		} else {
   498→			slog.Info("project notification sent",
   499→				"channel", ch.Name, "project_id", run.ProjectID, "version", result.version)
   500→		}
   501→	}
   502→}
   503→
   504→// parseReport attempts to parse the agent's text output as a SemanticReport JSON.
   505→// It handles cases where the agent wraps the JSON in markdown code blocks.
   506→func parseReport(text string) (*models.SemanticReport, error) {
   507→	// Strip markdown code fences if present.
   508→	cleaned := strings.TrimSpace(text)
   509→	if strings.HasPrefix(cleaned, "```") {
   510→		// Remove opening fence (with optional language tag).
   511→		if idx := strings.Index(cleaned, "\n"); idx != -1 {
   512→			cleaned = cleaned[idx+1:]
   513→		}
   514→		// Remove closing fence.
   515→		if idx := strings.LastIndex(cleaned, "```"); idx != -1 {
   516→			cleaned = cleaned[:idx]
   517→		}
   518→		cleaned = strings.TrimSpace(cleaned)
   519→	}
   520→
   521→	var report models.SemanticReport
   522→	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(cleaned), &report); err != nil {
   523→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse report JSON: %w", err)
   524→	}
   525→
   526→	if report.Subject == "" && report.Summary == "" {
   527→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("report is missing required 'subject' or 'summary' field")
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	return &report, nil
   531→}
   532→

     3→import (
     4→	"context"

     6→
     7→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool"
     8→	"google.golang.org/adk/tool/functiontool"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    11→)
    12→
    13→// AgentDataStore defines the data access methods that agent tools use to
    14→// research releases and context sources for a given project.
    15→type AgentDataStore interface {
    16→	ListReleasesByProject(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int, includeExcluded bool) ([]models.Release, int, error)
    17→	GetRelease(ctx context.Context, id string) (*models.Release, error)
    18→	ListContextSources(ctx context.Context, projectID string, page, perPage int) ([]models.ContextSource, int, error)
    19→}
    20→
    21→// --- Tool input/output types ---
    22→
    23→// GetReleasesInput is the input for the get_releases tool.
    24→type GetReleasesInput struct {
    25→	Page    int `json:"page,omitempty"`
    26→	PerPage int `json:"per_page,omitempty"`
    27→}
    28→
    29→// ReleaseItem is a single release in the tool output.
    30→type ReleaseItem struct {
    31→	ID         string `json:"id"`
    32→	SourceID   string `json:"source_id"`
    33→	Version    string `json:"version"`
    34→	RawData    any    `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    35→	ReleasedAt string `json:"released_at,omitempty"`
    36→	CreatedAt  string `json:"created_at"`
    37→}
    38→
    39→// GetReleasesOutput is the output for the get_releases tool.
    40→type GetReleasesOutput struct {
    41→	Releases []ReleaseItem `json:"releases"`
    42→	Total    int           `json:"total"`
    43→	Page     int           `json:"page"`
    44→	PerPage  int           `json:"per_page"`
    45→}
    46→
    47→// GetReleaseDetailInput is the input for the get_release_detail tool.
    48→type GetReleaseDetailInput struct {
    49→	ReleaseID string `json:"release_id"`
    50→}
    51→
    52→// GetReleaseDetailOutput is the output for the get_release_detail tool.
    53→type GetReleaseDetailOutput struct {
    54→	ID         string `json:"id"`
    55→	SourceID   string `json:"source_id"`
    56→	Version    string `json:"version"`
    57→	RawData    any    `json:"raw_data,omitempty"`
    58→	ReleasedAt string `json:"released_at,omitempty"`
    59→	CreatedAt  string `json:"created_at"`
    60→}
    61→
    62→// ListContextSourcesInput is the input for the list_context_sources tool.
    63→type ListContextSourcesInput struct {
    64→	Page    int `json:"page,omitempty"`
    65→	PerPage int `json:"per_page,omitempty"`
    66→}
    67→
    68→// ContextSourceItem is a single context source in the tool output.
    69→type ContextSourceItem struct {
    70→	ID     string `json:"id"`
    71→	Type   string `json:"type"`
    72→	Name   string `json:"name"`
    73→	Config any    `json:"config"`
    74→}
    75→
    76→// ListContextSourcesOutput is the output for the list_context_sources tool.
    77→type ListContextSourcesOutput struct {
    78→	Sources []ContextSourceItem `json:"sources"`
    79→	Total   int                 `json:"total"`
    80→	Page    int                 `json:"page"`
    81→	PerPage int                 `json:"per_page"`
    82→}
    83→
    84→// toolFactory holds the store reference and project ID scoped to a single
    85→// agent run. Tools created from this factory are bound to a specific project.
    86→type toolFactory struct {
    87→	store     AgentDataStore
    88→	projectID string
    89→}
    90→
    91→// NewTools creates the ADK function tools for agent research, scoped to a
    92→// specific project. The returned tools use the provided store to query
    93→// releases and context sources belonging to projectID.
    94→func NewTools(store AgentDataStore, projectID string) ([]tool.Tool, error) {
    95→	f := &toolFactory{store: store, projectID: projectID}
    96→
    97→	getReleases, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
    98→		Name:        "get_releases",
    99→		Description: "Fetch recent releases for the current project. Returns a paginated list of releases with version, raw data, and timestamps. Use this to understand what new versions have been published.",
   100→	}, f.getReleases)
   101→	if err != nil {
   102→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create get_releases tool: %w", err)
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	getReleaseDetail, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
   106→		Name:        "get_release_detail",
   107→		Description: "Get detailed information for a specific release by its ID. Returns the full raw data payload from the upstream provider. Use this to inspect changelogs, commit lists, or other release metadata.",
   108→	}, f.getReleaseDetail)
   109→	if err != nil {
   110→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create get_release_detail tool: %w", err)
   111→	}
   112→
   113→	listContextSources, err := functiontool.New(functiontool.Config{
   114→		Name:        "list_context_sources",
   115→		Description: "List the context sources configured for this project. Context sources are references to runbooks, documentation, monitoring dashboards, and other background materials that help you produce richer analysis.",
   116→	}, f.listContextSources)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("create list_context_sources tool: %w", err)
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	return []tool.Tool{getReleases, getReleaseDetail, listContextSources}, nil
   122→}
   123→
   124→// getReleases is the handler for the get_releases tool.
   125→func (f *toolFactory) getReleases(ctx tool.Context, input GetReleasesInput) (GetReleasesOutput, error) {
   126→	page := input.Page
   127→	if page < 1 {
   128→		page = 1
   129→	}
   130→	perPage := input.PerPage
   131→	if perPage < 1 || perPage > 50 {
   132→		perPage = 20
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	releases, total, err := f.store.ListReleasesByProject(ctx, f.projectID, page, perPage, false)
   136→	if err != nil {
   137→		return GetReleasesOutput{}, fmt.Errorf("list releases: %w", err)
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	items := make([]ReleaseItem, 0, len(releases))
   141→	for _, r := range releases {
   142→		item := ReleaseItem{
   143→			ID:        r.ID,
   144→			SourceID:  r.SourceID,
   145→			Version:   r.Version,
   146→			RawData:   r.RawData,
   147→			CreatedAt: r.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z"),
   148→		}
   149→		if r.ReleasedAt != nil {
   150→			item.ReleasedAt = r.ReleasedAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z")
